data.title: DOJ opens criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Powell in pressure to slash interest rates, escalating political battle.
data.description: The recent development of the Justice Department initiating a criminal investigation into Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, marks a significant escalation in efforts to push the central bank to reduce interest rates. This decision underscores the intensifying pressure on Powell and highlights the seriousness of the situation. The move comes amidst ongoing debates regarding interest rate adjustments and their impact on the economy. Powell's handling of monetary policy has been a topic of contention, with critics urging for more aggressive rate cuts to stimulate economic growth. The decision to investigate Powell reflects the growing concerns and disagreements surrounding the Federal Reserve's approach to interest rates. It signifies a crucial moment in the relationship between the government and the central bank, with potential implications for the wider financial landscape. This development has generated significant attention and speculation within political and economic circles, with stakeholders closely monitoring the situation for further updates.

data.title: American Skin Association welcomes Dr. Steven A. Rosenberg, a leading figure in cancer immunotherapy, to its Board of Directors.
data.description: On January 12, 2026, the American Skin Association (ASA) welcomed Dr. Steven A. Rosenberg, a leading expert in cancer immunotherapy, to its esteemed Board of Directors. His appointment brings added expertise to the board, which oversees vital initiatives in skin health and research.

data.title: Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson lost $2.5 billion in 4 years due to government involvement in crypto, industry chaos.
data.description: Charles Hoskinson, the founder of Cardano (ADA) revealed a significant loss of over $2.5 billion in the last four years. He attributed the decline in the cryptocurrency market not to technological shortcomings but to government interference. The industry had anticipated a bull market following Trump's election, but instead, chaos ensued with most cryptocurrencies plummeting by 40-50% during his term. Hoskinson humorously likened the current state of crypto to Japan in 1946. Despite surviving events like the FTX collapse, Luna implosion, and Gary Gensler's SEC enforcement, the industry faced new challenges due to government interventions. Hoskinson criticized the creation of the Trump memecoin, labeling it as "catastrophic" and detrimental to bipartisan support. This commentary by Hoskinson sheds light on the adverse impact of government involvement on the cryptocurrency market's health and stability.

data.title: DOJ opens criminal probe of Federal Reserve chair Powell, escalating pressure to cut rates in political battle.
data.description: The announcement of a criminal investigation into Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, by the Justice Department marks a significant increase in pressure on the central bank to reduce interest rates. This development underscores the seriousness of the situation and the heightened scrutiny surrounding Powell's actions. The move is seen as a bold maneuver in the ongoing effort to push for interest rate cuts, with the potential to have far-reaching implications on the Federal Reserve's policies. This decision reflects a clear escalation in efforts to influence the central bank's decision-making process.

data.title: Hungry Howie's ends 2025 with growth, innovation, and awards, expanding footprint and impact. - 120 characters
data.description: A well-known pizza franchise achieved significant growth in 2025 through strategic expansion, innovative menu offerings, and community engagement. Hungry Howie's, based in Madison Heights, Michigan, reported a successful year with a focus on increasing its presence. Notably, the franchise is the pioneer of Flavored Crust® pizza. The company's efforts led to the closure of 2025 on a positive note, with strong momentum for the future.

data.title: Cancer cure research is progressing fast with a projected $69.16 billion industry by 2032. Speedy advancements by 2026.
data.description: A news release from Oncolytics Biotech Inc. in Vancouver, BC, on Jan. 12, 2026, discusses the rapid regulatory developments in the oncology field. The integration of genomics into cancer treatment is expected to grow significantly, with projections indicating a potential market value of $69.16 billion by 2032. This marks a substantial shift in the oncology landscape, indicating the increasing significance of genomics in cancer care.

data.title: Travel club Travelzoo offers new Club Offers in Canada: $1299 for Madrid & Barcelona 6-night package.
data.description: In Toronto on January 12, 2026, Travelzoo® disclosed three Club Offers exclusively available for Club Members in Canada. The offers include a package for $1299 for a 6-night trip to Madrid and Barcelona, inclusive of flights. The deals are specially selected and bargained for by Travelzoo® to cater to travel enthusiasts.

data.title: MY DR NOW opens new clinic in Chandler on Jan. 12, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. at Albertsons shopping center.
data.description: Chandler, Arizona - On January 12, 2026, MY DR NOW, a leading primary care provider in the United States, is set to launch a fresh family medicine clinic in South Chandler at 8:00 a.m. The clinic will be situated in the Albertsons shopping center on the southwest corner.

data.title: HealthLeap appoints new executive leadership to accelerate growth in 2026, aiming to improve patient outcomes.
data.description: HealthLeap, a leading AI platform focused on enhancing patient outcomes and hospital profitability, has strengthened its team by onboarding seasoned healthcare and AI professionals. The company, headquartered in San Francisco, revealed on January 12, 2026, its plan to expand operations and advance its goal of improving patient care. The platform assists clinicians in prioritizing and managing patient treatment effectively.

data.title: OutSystems appoints Fay Sien Goon as CFO to oversee financial operations, strategy for AI dev platform.
data.description: OutSystems, a prominent AI development platform based in Boston, has recently named Fay Sien Goon as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO). In her capacity as CFO, Goon will be responsible for managing the company's worldwide financial activities, planning, and strategic direction. This appointment comes at a critical juncture as OutSystems aims to further solidify its position in the market and drive advancements in AI applications and agents. Fay Sien brings with her extensive expertise from scaling successful firms within the technology sector, signaling a strong addition to the OutSystems leadership team.

data.title: Multiply Labs partners with NVIDIA for advanced biomanufacturing using robotics technology, aiming to scale production of cell and gene therapies.
data.description: In San Francisco, Multiply Labs revealed a significant achievement in advancing the production of cell and gene therapies. The company, recognized for its prowess in robotic biomanufacturing, has embraced NVIDIA's Isaac and GR00T technologies to enhance its operations. By incorporating cutting-edge robotics simulation and perception tools, Multiply Labs has ushered in a new era for the industry, departing from traditional manual methods to a more automated approach. This shift comes at a crucial time as cell and gene therapies, known for their transformative impact, are gaining momentum in the medical field.

data.title: Report on Co-Packaged Optics Market 2026-2036 by ResearchAndMarkets.com, analyzing CPO industry's impact on data center interconnect architecture for AI/ML workloads.
data.description: Dublin--ResearchAndMarkets.com has recently included "The Global Co-Packaged Optics Market 2026-2036" report in its offerings. This report provides an in-depth analysis of the Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) market, focusing on the period of 2026-2036. It delves into the growing CPO industry, highlighting its impact on data center interconnect architecture to address the increasing bandwidth requirements driven by artificial intelligence and machine learning tasks. The report explores how hyperscale operators and AI infrastructure are adapting to this transformative technology.

data.title: Caswell™ Live Fire spins off from InVeris™, celebrates 100th anniversary. Founded in 1926, known for excellence in shooting range technology.
data.description: Atlanta-based company Caswell™ Live Fire, a renowned leader in shooting range design and services, has formally separated from its parent company InVeris™ as it celebrates its 100th anniversary since its establishment. The significant event signifies a fresh beginning for Caswell as it transitions into a phase of independence and advancement within the live fire technology sector. Founded in 1926, Caswell has been at the forefront of setting worldwide benchmarks for safety in shooting ranges. This move marks a new era for the company with a century-long history of excellence.

data.title: Innospec to release Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 17 after market close, conference call on Feb 18, ET 9:00 am with company executives.
data.description: Innospec Inc., a company listed under NASDAQ as IOSP, recently revealed its plan to unveil its earnings report for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 17, 2026. The announcement is slotted to occur after the market closes. Subsequently, on February 18, 2026, there will be an interactive conference call at 9:00 a.m. ET. This session will be hosted by Patrick S. Williams, the President and Chief Executive Officer, and Ian Cleminson, the Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer of Innospec Inc.

data.title: BridgeBio reveals Q4 2025 product revenue at $146M & full year at $362.4M at J.P. Morgan Conference. Milestones set for 2026.
data.description: In the fourth quarter of 2025, preliminary unaudited figures show that net Attruby ® product revenue reached $146.0 million. Additionally, for the full year of 2025, the net product revenue is reported at $362.4 million.

data.title: Inventor from Las Cruces, N.M. creates DOOR LATCH SYSTEM to secure school doors in seconds during lockdown situation.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 12, 2026, an inventor from Las Cruces, New Mexico, shared insights on the need for improved safety measures during school lockdowns. The inventor introduced the innovative DOOR LATCH SYSTEM, aiming to provide a more effective method for teachers to quickly secure doors in emergency situations. This invention addresses critical seconds in lockdown scenarios and offers a potential solution to enhance security protocols.
